task_completion.description: Diagram contains four horizontal swimlanes with the correct labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a cross-functional flowchart structure in draw.io with exactly four horizontal lanes labeled 'House of Representatives', 'Senate', 'Conference Committee', and 'President'.

task_completion.description: Diagram contains at least 14 process steps distributed across the swimlanes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the process boxes (typically rectangles) inside the swimlanes. There must be at least 14 distinct steps covering introduction, committee referral, hearings, floor debate, voting, and reconciliation.

task_completion.description: Diagram includes at least 3 decision points and start/end terminators.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 3 diamond shapes representing decisions (e.g., vote outcomes, version matching, sign/veto) and oval or rounded-rectangle shapes representing the Start and End states of the process.

task_completion.description: A second page titled 'Veto Override' is created showing the override process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the page tabs at the bottom of the draw.io interface. There should be a second tab named 'Veto Override' containing its own diagram elements.

task_completion.description: The draw.io source file is saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io application title bar showing 'legislation_process.drawio' or a file explorer/Desktop view showing the 'legislation_process.drawio' file.

task_completion.description: Page 1 of the diagram is exported as a PNG image to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog confirming the PNG save, an image viewer displaying the exported diagram, or a file named 'legislation_process.png' visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io application open with the diagram being constructed using its tools, rather than a text editor writing raw XML/drawio code.

integrity.description: The diagram uses proper shapes and connectors rather than a single pasted text block.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the diagram consists of distinct shapes (rectangles, diamonds, ovals) connected by arrow lines, rather than a single large text box containing the entire process description.

integrity.description: The PNG file was genuinely exported from the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the agent using the File > Export as > PNG menu in draw.io, rather than just taking a generic screenshot tool and renaming it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The diagram must have exactly 4 horizontal swimlanes (House of Representatives, Senate, Conference Committee, President). It must contain at least 14 process steps (rectangles) and at least 3 decision points (diamonds) covering vote outcomes, version matching, and presidential actions. It must have start and end terminators. A second page titled 'Veto Override' must exist. The final outputs must be saved as 'legislation_process.drawio' and 'legislation_process.png' on the Desktop. Required keywords that should appear across the diagram: house, senate, president, conference, committee, vote, veto, bill, law.
